stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Easter truce expires as both sides accuse the other of violations
stories.summary: Ukraine records more than 2,000 violations, Russia claims 1,900. Zelenskyy congratulates Hungary’s Péter Magyar. What we know on day 1,510 A ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine to mark the Orthodox Easter formally expired on Monday , with both sides having accused each other of thousands of violations, despite a lull in Russian air raids. The truce lasted 32 hours, from 4pm (1300 GMT) on Saturday until the end of the day on Sunday. Both sides had agreed to observe the ceasefire, which the Russi

stories.title: Orbán’s 16-year rule over Hungary ends in crushing election defeat
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — The 16-year reign of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán is at an end after a crushing election loss on Sunday that will send political shockwaves from Washington to Moscow.   The EU’s most autocratic leader — a close ally of both U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin — lost by a decisive margin in Sunday’s vote, amid the highest turnout in Hungary’s democratic history. With almost all of the votes counted , his opponent Péter Magyar looked set

stories.title: Bulgaria’s elections are not for sale, PM insists as he vows crackdown on tampering
stories.summary: Bulgaria’s Prime Minister Andrey Gurov is promising tougher action against vote-buying and election manipulation as the country heads into its eighth parliamentary election in five years. The vote follows mass protests in December over accusations of entrenched government corruption and backsliding on the rule of law that toppled the center-right GERB-led coalition. Gurov, a deputy governor for the central bank and member of the anti-corruption party Continuing the Change, was appointed interim

stories.title: DHS investigating claim about Swalwell nanny filed by conspiracy theorist
stories.summary: Joel Gilbert, who mailed anti-Barack Obama film to voters in 2012, accuses congressman of violating immigration law Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox California Democrat Eric Swalwell’s decision to suspend his campaign for governor on Sunday, even as he denies allegations from four women who accuse him of sexual misconduct and assault, did not end the pressure the congressman faces. One sexual assault allegation against Swalwell, alleged to have been c

stories.title: Von der Leyen waits just 17 minutes to celebrate Orbán’s heavy defeat
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen rejoiced over Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s shattering loss in Sunday’s national election.  “Hungary has chosen Europe,” von der Leyen wrote on social media just 17 minutes after the Hungarian prime minister conceded defeat to opposition rival Péter Magyar. “Europe has always chosen Hungary. A country reclaims its European path. The Union grows stronger.”  Orbán was among von der Leyen’s most bi

stories.title: Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 'painful' election result - video
stories.summary: Orbán concedes defeat after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' election result, less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday. 'I congratulated the victorious party,' the rightwing populist told supporters in Budapest after a landslide win by Péter Magyar’s Tisza party. 'We are going to serve the Hungarian nation and our homeland from opposition as well,' he said Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Continue reading...

stories.title: Hope, fear and an ‘unpredictable outcome’ as Hungarians vote after 16 years of Orbán
stories.summary: Voters weigh change against stability as opposition mounts strongest challenge yet to long-serving prime minister As Fruzsi, 22, waited to cast her vote at a polling station in central Budapest, a note of optimism laced through her voice. “I think everyone is hopeful,” she said. “It feels like we might be writing history tonight.” It was a hint of the heady mix of hope, anxiety, and fear being felt across the country as a record number of Hungarians turned up on Sunday to cast their ballots in t

stories.title: Move to expel Democrat Eric Swalwell from Congress gains steam
stories.summary: Pressure mounts on Californian, who denies rape claims, and on Texan Tony Gonzales, who had affair with staffer who died by suicide Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A growing number of Congress members from both parties have called for Eric Swalwell, a Democratic US representative who ended his campaign for California governor on Sunday, to also resign his seat, following reported allegations of inappropriate behavior, sexual assault and rape. Swalwel

stories.title: Péter Magyar: Hungary’s next leader energised voters but is ‘a dark horse’
stories.summary: Ex-government insider turned Orbán critic is authentic and passionate but his proposed policies are short on details As a child growing up in Budapest, Péter Magyar had a poster of Viktor Orbán – at the time a leading figure in the country’s pro-democracy movement – hanging above his bed. Orbán was one of several political figures who adorned his bedroom, Magyar told a podcast last year, hinting at his excitement over the changes sweeping the country after the collapse of communism. Now Magyar,

stories.title: Magyar celebrates as Orbán’s 16-year rule ends - as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. You can read more about election night here Europe correspondent Not a regular observer of Hungarian politics? We’ve got you. Continue reading...

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán, 62, was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US as the mastermind of the "illiberal" model of democracy, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, internat

stories.title: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s Tisza party wins election as prime minister concedes defeat, in result likely to reshape ties with EU Hungary election live Hungary’s opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, has won the election, bringing an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, in a result that is likely to rattle the White House and reshape the country’s relationship with the EU. Less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday, Orbán conceded defeat after what he described as a “painful but unambi

stories.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16 years in power is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.title: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Russia expert: We know of at least three Russian government-affiliated organizations that are interfering in the Hungarian elections
stories.summary: András Rácz told Telex that he can't rule out Fidesz not accepting the election's result if it is unfavourable to them.

stories.title: Nem tudja megváltoztatni a trendet a Fidesz: sokkal több az új választó a nagyvárosokban
stories.summary: Április 12-én minden eddiginél izgalmasabb és kiélezettebb választást tartanak Magyarországon, a Telexen pedig természetesen kiemelten foglalkozunk a vasárnapi eseményekkel. Napközbeni élő közvetítésünket itt tudjátok visszanézni.

stories.title: People celebrate in streets of Budapest after Tisza’s landslide victory
Magyar Péter hallucináló állampártról beszélt, és közben bizakodva várja az eredményt
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint a megfigyelőik is hatékonyak voltak, mert szerinte kevesebb szavazatot tudott most vásárolni a Fidesz.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor elismerte a választási vereséget: Mi ellenzékből is a hazánkat fogjuk szolgálni
stories.summary: Élete legrövidebb beszédét mondta a leköszönő miniszterelnök.

stories.title: A legfontosabb választási eredmények röviden, egy helyen
stories.summary: Minden korábbinál nagyobb felhatalmazást kaptak Magyar Péterék vasárnap, a Fidesz legerősebb választókerületeit is átfordították.

stories.title: Vége van: 16 év után megbukott Orbán Viktor rendszere, Magyar Péter alakíthat kormányt
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt kétharmados többséget szerzett a választásokon, Magyar Péter lesz az új miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Lemond Dobrev és a DK teljes elnöksége
stories.summary: Azt mondta, hogy a felelősség innen Magyaré, és reméli, hogy a Tisza elnöke nem viseli tovább a bocskait, mert megosztónak tartja.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor: Ennek a képmutató, embertelen politikának véget kell vetni
stories.summary: Az Orbán Viktor idősebb gyermekeit egykor megkeresztelő lelkész reményei szerint véget ér az a politikai világ, ahol az MNB 600 milliárdját büntetlenül el lehet lopni.

stories.title: After sixteen years, Orbán's regime is over, Péter Magyar set to form government
stories.summary: The Tisza Party has won a two-thirds majority in Hungary's election, which Péter Magyar said they would use to restore Hungary to being a democratic state governed by the rule of law.

stories.title: Magyarország Európát választotta – sorban gratulálnak Magyar Péternek az európai vezetők és államfők
stories.summary: A német kancellár, a francia elnök, az Európai Bizottság elnöke és a brit miniszterelnök is kifejezte jókívánságait.

stories.title: Zelenszkij is gratulált Magyar Péternek
stories.summary: „Készen állunk a találkozókra és a közös konstruktív munkára mindkét nemzet javára”-írta az ukrán elnök az X-en.

stories.title: „Óráid vannak hátra, Orbán!” – kiáltotta valaki 2018-ban a köztévén. De mennyi is lett az pontosan?
stories.summary: 2018-ban valaki bekiabált a köztévé élő adásába Londonban, a jelenet azóta legendás lett. Választ kaptunk a kettővel ezelőtti választás legnagyobb kérdésére!

stories.title: Magyar Péter győzelmi beszédében elszámoltatást és megbékélést ígért, és lemondásra szólította fel Sulyok Tamást
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint minél hamarabb át akarják venni a kormányzást, ami után úgy akarnak majd kormányozni, hogy azzal a fideszesek is elégedettek legyenek. A kétharmados felhatalmazást elmondása szerint egy „működő és emberséges” Magyarország létrehozására akarják fordítani.

stories.title: Donald Tusk is üdvözölte a Tisza győzelmét
stories.summary: Magyarország-Lengyelország-EU újra együtt! – írta a lengyel miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Mráz Ágoston a köztévén arról értekezett, hogy Magyar győzelme egy flash mob, egy villámcsődület, ami egy pillanatra összeállt
stories.summary: Az M1 műsorában sokáig nem merték megmutatni a teljes állást, de Sárvár történelméről meg a szarvasbőgésről is beszélgettek.

stories.title: Így nézett ki a nap, amikor a Tisza Párt elsöpörte Orbán Viktor 16 éve fennálló rendszerét
stories.summary: 2026. április 12-e, Magyarország 10. szabad választása óriási ellenzéki győzelmet hozott. Fotósaink legjobb képeivel mutatjuk be, hogy zajlott a nap a két versengő pártnál, és hogyan ünnepelte Budapest Magyar Péterék diadalát.

stories.title: „Történelmi hatalomváltás Magyarországon” – így reagált a nemzetközi sajtó a Tisza győzelmére
stories.summary: Londontól New Yorkig, Madridtól Moszkváig címlapokon és kiemelt helyen számoltak be a külföldi lapok Orbán bukásáról.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: Hungarians speak to the Guardian before decisive election – video
stories.summary: Voters will head to the polls on Sunday in what is seen as Europe’s most consequential election of the year. The Guardian's Jakub Krupa and Flora Garamvolgyi speak to Hungarians in Budapest before the vote Continue reading...

stories.title: A kormánymédia már ott tart, hogy a Tisza a választás estéjén Majdanná változtatná Budapestet, Magyar szerint ez csak fideszes rémhír
stories.summary: Egy „kiszivárgott belsős akcióterv” alapján arról cikkeznek, vereség esetén a Tisza szervezetten megindulna a kormányzati épületek ellen, Magyar Péter szerint ez csak „erőtlen és kétségbeesett hangulatkeltés”.

stories.title: Rácz András: Legalább három orosz kormányközeli szervről tudunk, ami beavatkozik a magyar választásokba
stories.summary: Az Oroszország-szakértő szerint elképzelhető, hogy a Fidesz nem fogja elfogadni a választás eredményét.

stories.title: Magyar Péter kétmillió lájkot vert Orbánra a kampányhajrában
stories.summary: A maximumra pörgették mind a ketten a Facebookot, Magyarnál a kampánygyűlések futottak nagyot, Orbánnál a húsvéti sonka meg a locsolkodás.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Iran warns move would breach ceasefire as US president also repeats threats to strike critical infrastructure Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has said the US will begin blockading the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to take control of the strategic waterway from Iran in the aftermath of failed peace negotiations between the countries in Pakistan. The US president also threatened to bomb Iran’s water treatment facilities, power plants and bridges if Tehran did not agree to abandon i

stories.title: Ireland cuts tax on motor fuel as protests grip the country
stories.summary: DUBLIN — Ireland’s government announced more than €500 million in tax cuts on motor fuel on Sunday in an attempt to placate protesters snarling key ports and roadways. Prime Minister Micheál Martin announced the cuts hours after police successfully cleared protesters from the ports of Galway and Foynes in western Ireland as well as on Dublin’s central boulevard, O’Connell Street, which tractors and trucks had blocked since Tuesday. The clearances came a day after police backed by soldiers broke

stories.title: The Iran war has been good for Starmer — to a point
stories.summary: Mujtaba Rahman is the head of Eurasia Group’s Europe practice. He posts at @Mij_Europe . The war in Iran has improved U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s chances of remaining in office. Make no mistake, his underlying position is still weak. Yet as the tenuous ceasefire looks like it may unravel, the likelihood of Starmer facing a leadership challenge after the May 7 regional elections to the Scottish and Welsh parliaments and English councils is falling. In fact, Labour members of parliame

stories.title: One dead and six wounded after mass shooting at Chick-fil-A in New Jersey
stories.summary: Officials say ‘no arrests made at this time’ and that shooting ‘does not appear to be a random act of violence’ Seven people were shot, including one fatally, at a fast-food chain restaurant in Union Township, New Jersey , on Saturday night, according to authorities. The Gun Violence Archive, a nonpartisan reference resource, listed the reported shooting at the Chick-fil-A restaurant in the 2300 block of Route 22 as the 100th mass shooting documented in the US this year, as of Sunday. The archiv

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns US blockade of strait of Hormuz would violate ceasefire

stories.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: The war with Iran offers a snapshot of Trump world. It’s not a pretty picture.
stories.summary: As the U.S. and Iran meet over the negotiating table in Pakistan this weekend, the outcome of Operation Epic Fury remains deeply uncertain. But one thing is clear: The war with Iran has offered a remarkably revealing snapshot of how the Trump administration really operates — inside the West Wing, across Washington and around the world. The picture that emerges is not a pretty one for President Donald Trump. The conflict has proved messier and more complex than Trump expected. The resulting energ

stories.title: Mauritius vows to ‘decolonise’ Chagos Islands after Starmer shelves handover
stories.summary: Mauritian foreign minister pledges to ‘spare no effort’ to regain control of islands, as US fails to give approval of deal A senior official in Mauritius’ government has vowed that the Chagos Islands will be “decolonised” after Keir Starmer was forced to shelve legislation to hand the islands back to Mauritius. On Friday, UK government officials acknowledged that they had run out of time to pass legislation within the current parliamentary session, which ends in the coming weeks, after a lack of

stories.title: Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly kills at least 100 civilians
stories.summary: Officials confirm misfire as Amnesty gives death toll after speaking to survivors of strike on market in Yobe state A Nigerian air force strike targeting jihadist rebels hit a market in north-east Nigeria, killing more than 100 people and injuring many others, Amnesty International and local media have said. Officials confirmed a misfire had occurred but did not provide details. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain could adopt single market rules without MPs’ vote as part of UK-EU reset
stories.summary: Exclusive: Ministers planning new legislation for alignment without full parliamentary scrutiny if in national interest Ministers are planning to fundamentally reshape Britain’s relationship with the European Union, with new legislation that could result in the UK signing up to EU single market rules without a normal parliamentary vote. In a major development in the prime minister’s push for closer ties with the continent after the Iran war, the Guardian understands ministers are bracing to face

stories.title: Irish police clear Dublin blockade staged by fuel price protesters
stories.summary: Hundreds of officers deployed to regain control of O’Connell Street on sixth day of protests by farmers and hauliers Police have cleared a blockade of central Dublin by farmers and hauliers who were protesting about fuel prices, signalling a possible end to six days of protests that have rocked Ireland. Mounted units and hundreds of officers regained control of O’Connell Street in a peaceful operation that emptied the thoroughfare of trucks and tractors on Sunday morning. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘This is not serious leadership’: Donald Trump and Marco Rubio watch UFC in Miami as Iran talks fail
stories.summary: President has long been a fan of mixed martial arts Ivanka and Donald Trump Jr also at event Donald Trump and US secretary of state Marco Rubio attended a UFC event in Miami night on Saturday night as peace talks with Iran failed on the other side of the world. Trump entered the Kaseya Center shortly after 9pm alongside several members of his family and UFC chief Dana White, who has been a supporter of the president since his first term. Seated nearby was Rubio as well as the US ambassador to In

stories.title: All Australian immigration detainees to be handcuffed while travelling, US company says after spate of escapes
stories.summary: Exclusive: Documents viewed by Guardian Australia reveal private prison contractor told staff ‘restraints must be used’ for all risk levels Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The American private prison company running Australia’s onshore immigration detention system has ordered that all detainees be handcuffed while travelling – regardless of whether they pose a risk – after a string of security lapses. More than a

stories.title: Israeli strike kills paramedic, says Lebanese Red Cross – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our live coverage continues here A post about an hour ago on the Israel Defense Forces Telegram channel claimed that overnight, the IDF “identified a rocket launcher positioned and ready to launch toward the State of Israel in the area of Jouaiyya in southern Lebanon”. Shortly after the identification, the launcher was struck and dismantled in a rapid closure cycle, thwarting the launch before it could be carried out. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy, Putin mark Easter amid truce breach allegations
stories.summary: Orthodox Christians have been celebrating Easter in both Ukraine and Russia, with both countries' armed forces accusing each other of breaching a ceasefire.

stories.title: Nigeria: Many dead after military bombs village market
stories.summary: An airstrike on a market in Yobe, in northeastern Nigeria, has left scores of people dead and wounded, though details remain unclear. The military said it conducted a "precision" strike on a known terrorist enclave.

stories.title: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
stories.summary: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

stories.title: Interest in EVs surges in Europe as fuel prices jump after Iran war
stories.summary: Demand at online marketplaces could settle at a new, higher normal, with the crisis leaving consumers ‘scarred’ Car buyers’ interest in electric cars has surged across Europe since the start of the war in Iran, as the rising cost of petrol highlights the cheaper power available from a plug. Online marketplaces in the UK, Germany, France and Spain reported huge increases in inquiries about electric vehicles since the start of the conflict in February. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK will not join any Trump blockade of strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: UK’s willingness to consider role in removing mines from strait is seen as distinct from Trump’s blockade proposal Middle East crisis – live updates The UK will not be involved in any blockade of the strait of Hormuz, the Guardian understands, after claims by Donald Trump on Sunday that the US would be blockading the waterway with the assistance of Nato allies. Speaking to Fox News, Trump said “it won’t take long to clean out the strait” and claimed “numerous countries are going to be helping us

stories.title: Fried nuggets and steamed sponges off menu in school food overhaul in England
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome first update of school food standards in 13 years, which aims to help lower obesity rates The government is to announce an overhaul to school food standards in England that will lead to calorific classics such as fish and chips and steamed sponges being banned. The new rules of the first major update to school food standards in 13 years will apply from September. They are part of efforts to lower the rates of childhood obesity, with data for 2024 released by the NHS in Januar

stories.title: Thousands of unpaid carers to face DWP repayment demands during overhaul
stories.summary: Ministers admit carer’s allowance penalties will continue while review of more than 200,000 cases is carried out Thousands of unpaid carers will continue to be hit with hefty and potentially unfair benefit repayment demands, it has emerged, as a government initiative gets under way to fix welfare injustices that have drawn comparison to the Post Office scandal. Ministers will on Monday launch an audit of more than 200,000 historical carer’s allowance benefit cases, with an estimated 25,000 carer

stories.title: Pauline Hanson says convicted rapist employed by One Nation has been sacked
stories.summary: James Paterson had called decision by Pauline Hanson’s party to rehire Sean Black ‘absolutely extraordinary’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson says she has sacked the convicted rapist who worked as One Nation’s campaign manager, but accused Coalition MPs of playing “gutter politics” by criticising her decision to rehire him. Sean Black, who in 2018 was jailed for rape and subsequently lost his appeal

stories.title: Eric Swalwell quits California governor race after sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: Democratic congressman, running to replace Gavin Newsom, has faced multiple accusations Representative Eric Swalwell, the Democratic frontrunner in the fiercely contested race to be governor of California, has suspended his campaign amid a series of sexual assault and misconduct allegations by a former staff member and at least three other women. The woman who worked for Swalwell said the California congressman had sexually assaulted her twice when she was too inebriated to consent, according to

stories.title: Susan Coyle to be first woman to lead Australian army in ‘deeply historic moment’
stories.summary: Appointment part of a raft of senior defence changes as current navy chief Mark Hammond promoted to chief of defence force Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Lt Gen Susan Coyle, an officer of more than three decades standing, will become the first woman to lead the Australian army. Coyle has been announced as the new chief of army, with her term beginning in July. Continue reading...

stories.title: Peruvians go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
stories.summary: Crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for president Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime , corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians. About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all

stories.title: Australia should set immigration targets to achieve a ‘stable temporary population’, report says
stories.summary: Experts say our preoccupation with net overseas migration figures has distracted from a more meaningful discussion on the ‘scale of temporariness’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia should set immigration targets to achieve a “stable temporary population” to address the ballooning number of non-permanent residents that has stretched the country’s public services and housing, a new report argues. Temporary

stories.title: Anthony Albanese urges Israel to stop Lebanon attacks that intensified during Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: PM tells Guardian Australia Hezbollah should cease reprisals and confirms Australia’s military surveillance aircraft will remain in region Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has urged Israel to stop its attacks on Lebanon and raised concern over its intensified military campaign on Beirut and the country’s south after the ceasefire in the Middle East. stories.title: US-Iran talks: What prevented a deal and what's next?
stories.summary: The United States and Iran concluded 21 hours of direct talks in Pakistan on Sunday without reaching a deal to end the war, casting uncertainty over a fragile two-week ceasefire.

stories.title: Peru general elections: No clear frontrunner, runoff likely
stories.summary: More than 30 candidates are in contention or the presidency, with no clear frontrunner going into Sunday's vote.

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Hacking women's political underrepresentation, Dutch-style
stories.summary: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

stories.title: Justin Bieber headlines Coachella with nostalgia-fuelled set
stories.summary: The star spent much of the gig singing along to YouTube videos of early hits like Baby and Never say Never.

stories.title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
stories.summary: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

stories.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work in Strait of Hormuz?

stories.title: Trump's blockade threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
stories.summary: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

stories.title: Frostbite is least of worries for Canada forces grappling with new Arctic reality
stories.summary: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, some 1,300 members of Canada's military spent two winter months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

stories.title: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour
stories.summary: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

stories.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
stories.summary: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

stories.title: Oil back above $100 as US to blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
stories.summary: The failure of negotiations at the weekend has raised concerns that the global energy crisis will deepen.

stories.title: Congressman Eric Swalwell quits California governor race amid sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Top Democratic allies intensified pressure on him to exit the gubernatorial race.

stories.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
stories.summary: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Co

stories.title: Benin holds presidential election four months after failed coup
stories.summary: As president Patrice Talon steps down after a decade, the west African country’s finance minister is favourite to win This Sunday, just four months after a failed coup, Benin heads to the polls for a presidential election that feels more like a coronation than a contest. Patrice Talon, the businessman turned politician who has been president since 2016, is ineligible to run again after serving two five-year terms. Continue reading...

stories.title: JD Vance says talks failed due to Iran’s refusal to give up nuclear programme
stories.summary: Iranian delegates in Islamabad say Washington needs to do more to win their trust if talks to resolve US-Iran conflict are to be successful Middle East crisis – live updates The US vice-president, JD Vance, has blamed the failure of marathon negotiations with Iran on the country’s refusal to abandon its nuclear weapons programme, while Iranian delegates have claimed Washington needs to do more to win their trust. Vance, who left Islamabad on Sunday morning after 21 hours of talks with Iranian of

stories.title: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti
stories.summary: Officials said many killed at popular tourist site were young, with more people reported injured or missing At least 30 people, many of them young, have died and dozens more are reported to have been injured after a crush at a mountaintop fortress in northern Haiti that is a popular tourist spot. Jean Henri Petit, the head of civil protection for the country’s Nord department, said the incident took place on Saturday at Citadelle Henry, also known as Citadelle Laferrière, a large 19th-century fo

stories.title: Planeloads of negotiators and too little time: US and Iran’s 21 hours of talks
stories.summary: The two sides turned up to test one another’s resolve. It was probably unrealistic to expect a dispute that has taken up years of discussion to be settled in one marathon session Middle East crisis – live updates It was as if the two delegations in the Iran-US peace talks in Islamabad hoped that the sheer number of negotiators flown into Pakistan could overcome the handicap of having only a finite number of hours in which to settle a 20-year dispute over Iran’s nuclear ambitions, now overlaid by

stories.title: What could come after a rules-based world order?
stories.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran was, for many, a sign that the rules-based world order, a set of principles developed after World War II, is collapsing. If that era is over, what could be next?

stories.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
stories.summary: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

stories.title: India news: Jaishankar meets UAE deputy PM to discuss Iran war
stories.summary: India is reaching out to its key economic partners in the Middle East to take stock of the impact of the Iran war and ensure smooth trade. Meanwhile, PM Modi is busy campaigning in West Bengal. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: Most Germans see decline in public manners
stories.summary: Most Germans think people's manners are getting worse, although most rate themselves as polite. Meawhile, there's trouble at the top of Europe's largest economy amid a dispute over fuel price relief. DW has more.

stories.title: China boosts ties with Taiwan after opposition leader visits
stories.summary: China said it would resume flights between Taiwan and more cities on the mainland, as well as easing bans on Taiwanese food and TV shows.

stories.title: India: Legendary playback singer Asha Bhosle dies at 92
stories.summary: Asha Bhosle was one of the world's most prolific recording artists, with some 11,000 songs. She is one of the most celebrated voices of Bollywood songs in Indian history.

stories.title: Haiti: More than 30 killed in stampede at historical fort
stories.summary: A deadly stampede broke out during an annual event to celebrate Citadelle Laferriere, a UNESCO-listed fort that is a symbol of Haiti's hard-won independence.

stories.title: Does age verification for social media help protect kids?
stories.summary: A number of countries want to bring in age restrictions for social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram. But can such bans really tackle the problems social media poses?

stories.title: Benin election: Finance minister Wadagni expected to win
stories.summary: Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the favourite to win after being endorsed by outgoing president Patrice Talon. Wadagni's only rival, Paul Hounkpe, has a much lower profile.

stories.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.title: 'We need real peace': Ukrainians have little faith in Easter truce
stories.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
stories.summary: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

stories.summary: A Választási földrajz elemzője szerint a rekordok napját éljük. A Telex élő műsorában Bódi azt mondta, azokban a választókerületekben csekélyebb a részvételi növekedés, ahol hagyományosan a Fidesz az erősebb.

stories.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

stories.summary: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. stories.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
stories.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

stories.summary: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

stories.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
stories.summary: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

stories.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patron The Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded. stories.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive

stories.title: Netanyahu says there is no ceasefire in Lebanon as Israel launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: Israeli PM says he will continue to attack Hezbollah ‘with full force’ after attacks that killed more than 300 people Middle East crisis – live updates Benjamin Netanyahu has said there is “no ceasefire in Lebanon” and Israel would continue “to strike Hezbollah with full force” as the country’s military launched fresh strikes. The Israeli prime minister’s remarks and latest attacks on what the IDF called “Hezbollah launch sites” came shortly after Donald Trump said he had asked Netanyahu to be m

stories.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria tighten relations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's visit to Syria was not only a diplomatic gesture but motivated by strategic considerations regarding Russia, once a strong supporter of the Middle Eastern state.

stories.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
stories.summary: Two-week ceasefire comes after Trump spoke to Pakistan’s leaders, with China also believed to be exerting influence over Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday barely an hour before Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire, with Tehran agreeing to temporarily reopen the strait of Hormuz. Israel also agreed to the ceasefire,

stories.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
stories.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
stories.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

stories.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
stories.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

stories.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
stories.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
stories.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
stories.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

stories.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
stories.summary: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
stories.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Biometric checks stalled again for cross-Channel travellers
stories.summary: Fears of Easter chaos over scaling up of new EU border system are eased, with no facial IDs for Eurotunnel and Eurostar passengers Passengers crossing the Channel from the UK to France will not face new biometric checks in the coming weeks, despite an imminent deadline for the complete implementation of the EU’s entry-exit system (EES), ports say. Airlines and airports across Europe have feared chaos over the Easter holidays. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July.
